exercises.title: What does CascadeType.REMOVE do to shared entities?
exercises.scenario: CascadeType.REMOVE on @ManyToMany causes accidental deletion of shared data.
exercises.expected_solution:
What happens:
  Hibernate cascades REMOVE from phone to each Category:
  → DELETE FROM categories WHERE id='ELECTRONICS'
  → DELETE FROM categories WHERE id='MOBILES'
  → DELETE FROM products WHERE id='P001'

Electronics and Mobiles categories are PERMANENTLY DELETED from the database!
All other products that belonged to these categories now have orphaned join table rows
or foreign key constraint violations.

Is this intended? NO — completely wrong.
  Deleting one product should never delete a shared category.
  10,000 other products reference Electronics; they all lose their category!

Correct cascade for @ManyToMany:
  cascade = {CascadeType.PERSIST, CascadeType.MERGE}
  → Deleting product: Hibernate removes join table rows only (correct)
  → Electronics and Mobiles categories remain untouched (correct)

test_cases.expected:
What happens:
  Hibernate cascades REMOVE from phone to each Category:
  → DELETE FROM categories WHERE id='ELECTRONICS'
  → DELETE FROM categories WHERE id='MOBILES'
  → DELETE FROM products WHERE id='P001'

Electronics and Mobiles categories are PERMANENTLY DELETED from the database!
All other products that belonged to these categories now have orphaned join table rows
or foreign key constraint violations.

Is this intended? NO — completely wrong.
  Deleting one product should never delete a shared category.
  10,000 other products reference Electronics; they all lose their category!

Correct cascade for @ManyToMany:
  cascade = {CascadeType.PERSIST, CascadeType.MERGE}
  → Deleting product: Hibernate removes join table rows only (correct)
  → Electronics and Mobiles categories remain untouched (correct)

hints: CascadeType.ALL = PERSIST + MERGE + REMOVE + REFRESH + DETACH
REMOVE on @ManyToMany: deletes target entities, not just join table rows
Correct: cascade={PERSIST,MERGE} — Hibernate removes join rows automatically on delete

exercises.title: Complete the @ManyToMany @JoinTable configuration
exercises.scenario: The owning side needs @JoinTable; the inverse side needs mappedBy.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete the User ↔ Role @ManyToMany relationship.

blanks.hint: Annotation that configures the join table
FK column pointing to the owning entity (User)
FK column pointing to the other entity (Role)
The field name on the owning side (User.roles)

hints: @JoinTable: on owning side (no mappedBy), configures the join table
mappedBy='roles': 'roles' is the field name on the User entity
joinColumns: FK to THIS entity; inverseJoinColumns: FK to the OTHER entity

exercises.title: Write a minimal @ManyToMany relationship
exercises.scenario: Students belong to multiple Courses; Courses have multiple Students.
exercises.instructions:
Write two @Entity classes:
1. Student (id UUID, name, email) with @ManyToMany Set<Course>
2. Course (id UUID, title, code UNIQUE) with @ManyToMany(mappedBy)
3. Correct cascade on Student (owning side)
4. @JoinTable: student_courses, student_id, course_id
hints: Owning side: has @JoinTable, no mappedBy
Non-owning: has mappedBy pointing to the field on the owning class
Set<> preferred over List<> for @ManyToMany: prevents duplicates

exercises.title: Bidirectional helper methods
exercises.scenario: Always update both sides of a bidirectional relationship.
exercises.instructions:
Add helper methods to the Student entity from Ex 3:
1. enroll(Course course) — adds to student's courses AND updates course's students
2. withdraw(Course course) — removes from both sides
3. Show why updating only one side causes stale in-memory state
4. Test: enroll, then check both student.getCourses() and course.getStudents()
hints: enroll: this.courses.add(course) AND course.getStudents().add(this)
Hibernate writes from owning side; in-memory needs both sides updated
Helper methods are the standard pattern for bidirectional consistency

exercises.title: @Embeddable for Address value object
exercises.scenario: @Embeddable stores value objects as columns in the parent table — no separate table.
exercises.instructions:
Write:
1. @Embeddable Address: street, city, pincode, state
2. @Embeddable Money: amount (BigDecimal), currency (String, default='INR')
3. @Entity Order that embeds both: shippingAddress + orderTotal (Money)
4. @AttributeOverrides to embed Money twice (orderTotal + discount)
5. Explain: which SQL columns go in the orders table?
hints: @AttributeOverrides: required when embedding the same @Embeddable twice
BigDecimal for Money: never use double for financial amounts
All embedded columns go into the SAME table as the owning entity

exercises.title: @ElementCollection for tags and images
exercises.scenario: @ElementCollection stores simple values in a separate table with no entity lifecycle.
exercises.instructions:
Add to the Flipkart Product @Entity:
1. @ElementCollection Set<String> tags → product_tags table (product_id, tag)
2. @ElementCollection List<String> imageUrls → product_images table (product_id, image_url, index)
3. @Embeddable ProductSpecification (specKey, specValue) as @ElementCollection
4. Show the DDL (CREATE TABLE) that Hibernate generates for each collection
hints: @OrderColumn: maintains insertion order for List (adds index column)
@CollectionTable: specifies the join table name and FK column
Set<String> tags: no order, uses SET (no duplicates); List<String>: ordered, @OrderColumn needed

exercises.title: @DataJpaTest for @ManyToMany
exercises.scenario: Verify that @ManyToMany with helper methods works correctly.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @DataJpaTest for Student ↔ Course @ManyToMany:
1. Seed 2 students, 3 courses
2. Enroll Aisha in Java101 and Python202
3. Enroll Raj in Java101 only
4. Assert: Java101 has 2 students
5. Assert: Aisha has 2 courses
6. Assert: deleting Aisha does NOT delete Java101 or Python202 courses

All assertions pass.
hints: testEm.clear() after seeding: evict L1 cache for honest assertions
deleteAisha: with cascade={PERSIST,MERGE}, courses NOT deleted — only join rows removed
loaded.getStudents().size(): requires loading lazy Set — must be in transaction

exercises.title: CascadeType.ALL vs orphanRemoval — when each is correct
exercises.scenario: Choose the right cascade for each parent-child relationship.
exercises.instructions:
For each relationship, state the correct cascade + orphanRemoval and explain:
1. Order → List<OrderItem> (an item exists ONLY as part of one order)
2. Product → Set<Category> (categories are shared across products)
3. User → Address (address belongs to one user)
4. Blog → List<Comment> (comments belong to one post)
5. Playlist → List<Song> (songs are shared across playlists)

Write the @OneToMany or @ManyToMany with correct cascade + orphanRemoval for each.
hints: Exclusive ownership → CascadeType.ALL + orphanRemoval=true
Shared reference → cascade={PERSIST,MERGE} only, NO orphanRemoval
Value object → @Embeddable, no cascade needed at all

exercises.title: Soft delete with @Where and @SQLDelete
exercises.scenario: Soft delete: mark records as deleted without removing from DB.
exercises.instructions:
Implement soft delete for the Order @Entity:
1. Add deleted boolean field (default false)
2. @Where annotation: Hibernate adds WHERE deleted=false to all queries
3. @SQLDelete: intercepts DELETE to execute UPDATE SET deleted=true instead
4. softDelete() method on the entity
5. @DataJpaTest (3 tests): soft delete hides record, findAll count drops, DB row still exists (via native query)
hints: @SQLDelete: intercepts repo.deleteById() → runs UPDATE SET deleted=true instead
@Where: Hibernate appends WHERE deleted=false to ALL JPA queries
Native query bypasses @Where — useful for admin/audit views of soft-deleted data

exercises.title: What happens when you embed Address twice without @AttributeOverrides?
exercises.scenario: Embedding the same @Embeddable twice without column rename causes an error.
hints: Two @Embedded of same type = duplicate column names → MappingException
@AttributeOverrides: rename every column for the second embedding
Prefix pattern: 'pickup_' and 'delivery_' make column purpose clear

exercises.title: Auditing with Spring Data @CreatedDate @LastModifiedDate
exercises.scenario: Spring Data automatically sets created/updated timestamps on entities.
exercises.instructions:
Add auditing to the Product @Entity:
1. @EnableJpaAuditing on @SpringBootApplication
2. @EntityListeners(AuditingEntityListener.class) on Product
3. @CreatedDate LocalDateTime createdAt
4. @LastModifiedDate LocalDateTime updatedAt
5. @CreatedBy String createdBy + @LastModifiedBy String modifiedBy
6. AuditorAware<String> @Bean returning current user
7. @DataJpaTest: save product, verify createdAt is set, modify and verify updatedAt changes
hints: @EntityListeners(AuditingEntityListener.class): wires Spring audit listener to this entity
@CreatedDate updatable=false: timestamp set once on insert, never updated
AuditorAware: Spring calls this to get the current user string for @CreatedBy

exercises.title: Complete @ManyToMany with extra join table columns
exercises.scenario: Sometimes the join table itself needs extra data — use an explicit join entity.
exercises.instructions:
Student ↔ Course enrollment needs an 'enrolled_at' timestamp and a 'grade' in the join table.

Since @ManyToMany doesn't support extra join table columns, use an explicit @Entity:
1. Enrollment @Entity: student @ManyToOne, course @ManyToOne, enrolledAt, grade
2. Student has @OneToMany List<Enrollment> enrollments
3. EnrollmentService.enroll(studentId, courseId) creates the join record
4. @DataJpaTest (2 tests): enroll creates Enrollment, grade can be set
hints: When join table needs extra columns: use explicit @Entity, not @ManyToMany
Enrollment has @ManyToOne Student and @ManyToOne Course — two FK columns in join table
Student has @OneToMany Enrollment (mappedBy='student')

exercises.title: Full Flipkart product catalog — @ManyToMany + @Embeddable + soft delete
exercises.scenario: Complete domain model for Flipkart product catalog.
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete Flipkart product catalog:
1. Category @Entity (id, name UNIQUE, parentCategory nullable self-reference)
2. Product @Entity: name, @ManyToMany Set<Category>, @Embedded WarehouseCity, @ElementCollection Set<String> tags, soft delete (@Where + @SQLDelete)
3. ProductService: addProduct, addCategory, removeCategory, softDelete
4. @DataJpaTest (4 tests): add categories, remove category (categories not deleted), soft delete hides product, tags work

All 4 tests pass.
exercises.starter_code:
// Complete Flipkart product catalog
// Category (with self-reference), Product, ProductService, @DataJpaTest 4 tests
hints: Self-referential @ManyToOne: FCategory parent — parent is nullable (null = root)
@SQLDelete intercepts deleteById() → runs UPDATE; @Where hides the deleted row
flush() + clear() after mutations: forces DB write and clears L1 cache

exercises.title: Razorpay user role management — @ManyToMany with service operations
exercises.scenario: Role-based access control using @ManyToMany with business operations.
exercises.instructions:
Build Razorpay user-role management:
1. User @Entity (id, email, name) with @ManyToMany Set<Role> roles
2. Role @Entity (id, name UNIQUE, description)
3. UserRoleService: assignRole, revokeRole, hasPermission(userId, roleName)
4. Both using helper methods for bidirectional consistency
5. @DataJpaTest (3 tests): assign role, revoke doesn't delete role, hasPermission
exercises.starter_code:
// Razorpay user role management
// User, Role @Entity, UserRoleService, @DataJpaTest 3 tests
hints: addRole helper updates both user.roles and role.users for in-session consistency
revokeRole cascade={PERSIST,MERGE}: remove from collection removes join row only, role entity untouched
hasPermission: loads user entity → checks roles set (lazy-loaded)

exercises.title: Complete HDFC loan system — advanced relationships synthesis
exercises.scenario: Multi-entity JPA domain model with all advanced relationship types.
exercises.instructions:
Build HDFC bank loan system:
1. @Embeddable Money(amount BigDecimal, currency), @Embeddable Address
2. Customer @Entity: name, @Embedded homeAddress, @Embedded workAddress (@AttributeOverrides), @ManyToMany Set<LoanProduct>
3. LoanProduct @Entity: name, type, minAmount (Money), maxAmount (Money)
4. Loan @Entity: customer @ManyToOne, loanProduct @ManyToOne, sanctionedAmount (Money), @OneToMany List<Repayment> (cascade=ALL, orphanRemoval), @ElementCollection Set<String> documents
5. Repayment @Entity: amount (Money), paidAt, status
6. @DataJpaTest (3 tests): create loan with repayments, remove repayment (orphanRemoval deletes it), add documents

All 3 tests pass.
exercises.starter_code: // HDFC loan system — complete advanced JPA domain model
hints: @AttributeOverrides needed for each @Embedded Money (minAmount vs maxAmount columns)
orphanRemoval: repayments.clear() then save → DELETE all repayment rows
@ElementCollection documents: stored in h_loan_docs table
